record: GBI ITS-discovery pilot (capability A) — positive vs negative
capability: A (discovery: recover fungal ITS from SRA reads, classify vs UNITE)

reference_db.name: UNITE general FASTA release for Fungi

pipeline.bait: bbduk in=stdin.fq int=f ref=resources/rrna_seeds.fa k=31 hdist=1 minlength=100 threads=4
pipeline.discriminator_note: Fungal vs non-fungal is decided by BLAST identity to UNITE on ITSx-extracted ITS1/ITS2, NOT by sintax k:Fungi (which labels all queries Fungi against a Fungi-only reference).

headline: Alternaria ITS present in Silene (D28 positive), absent in Streptanthus (D28 negative) — independent confirmation of D28 via a different molecule, data type, and method.
limits: n=2 controlled pilot, not the 10-dataset population test
shared dominant genera across both unrelated hosts suggest a reagent/environmental/index-hop background; source undetermined (per D28)
Alternaria low-abundance (8 ITS, size=1); index-hopping not fully excluded but positive/negative specificity argues against it
does not establish residency (endophyte vs surface vs lab)
notes_engineering: fasterq-dump --stdout hangs from remote (2.11.3) and needs ~193GB scratch locally -> use prefetch + fastq-dump stream from local .sra
bbduk stdin FASTQ reader crashes at threads=16 (multithreaded record misalignment) -> threads=4 for stdin baiting
